data.title: Nike poised for 'healthy, profitable' growth by the end of 2025 - analyst
data.description:
Chief executive Elliott Hill’s “intensified urgency” to turn Nike (NYSE:NKE) around will take time – by the company’s own admission – and the elongated timeline is one of the main drags on the stock price. But Piper Sandler analyst Anna Andreeva thinks the company can return to healthy, profitable growth in 3–4 quarters, and although the market is more competitive with On Running (ONON), HOKA (DECK), New Balance and Asics, Nike (NYSE:NKE) has been able to pull off a turnaround before.

While buy-side sentiment remains negative (the number of shorted shares increased by 30% since September), Andreeva’s confidence in Nike’s (NKE) ability to come back resulted in an upgrade to Overweight from Neutral and a 25% hike to its price target to $90.

The forecasts for “healthy, profitable growth” hinges on management’s ability to create a full-price/off-price sales mix on direct-to-consumer digital, and its managed decline of key classic footwear franchises. And while it’s still early, there are some green shoots in product execution in the second quarter of 2025, namely sport performance, training, global football and baseball, all of which were up, while running turned positive in the last two quarters, offsetting a decline in sportswear.

For FY26, Andreeva forecasts gross margin expansion to be fueled by several factors: higher Nike (NKE) brand average selling price (footwear mix, less discounting, strategic pricing); higher margins in Nike Direct, off-price, and Converse; and, inventory obsolescence reversion. This expansion will likely be partially offset by: higher Nike Brand product costs; and, supply chain deleverage from sales decline in the first half of 2026.

Wall Street analyst and Seeking Alpha authors are both bullish on Nike (NKE), rating the stock as a Buy, while Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating views the company as a Hold.

data.title: Mersana falls after early-stage trial data for lead cancer therapy
data.description:
Cancer drug developer Mersana Therapeutics (NASDAQ:MRSN) hit a 52-week low on Friday after sharing early clinical results from a Phase 1 trial for its lead candidate emiltatug ledadotin (Emi-Le) in solid tumors.

Based on a Dec. 13 data cut, Mersana (NASDAQ:MRSN) said Emi-Le, an antibody-drug conjugate, led to a confirmed objective response rate of 23% at intermediate doses up to 67.4 mg/m2 in patients expressing a high amount of tumor biomarker B7-H4 in their cancers.

The group, including those with B7-H4 high triple-negative breast cancer, had previously received at least one regimen of ADCs known as topoisomerase-1 inhibitors.

However, in terms of safety, 2.3%, 9.2%, and 12.3% of patients discontinued the trial or experienced dose reduction or delay in response to treatment-related adverse events, respectively. There were no Grade 4 or 5 TRAEs. Cambridge, Massachusetts-based MRSN added.

Given the positive data at intermediate doses, the company has decided to advance the 67.4 mg/m2 dosage administered once every four weeks into an expansion cohort of TNBC patients who had previously undergone one or more topo-1 ADC therapies.

Separately, Mersana (MRSN) announced that the U.S. FDA has granted an additional fast-track designation for Emi-Le as a treatment for HER2-negative or HER2-low advanced or metastatic breast cancer, including those with TNBC.

Previously, the company won the FDA’s fast-track tag for Emi-Le as a treatment for adults with advanced or metastatic recurrent TNBC.

data.title: WisdomTree AUM dipped 2.9% in December amid net outflows
data.description:
WisdomTree's (NYSE:WT) assets under management retreated 2.9% to $109.8B at the end of December from $113.1B at the beginning of the month, the company said on Friday.

The decrease came as WisdomTree (NYSE:WT) endured $214M of net outflows during the month.

Inflows in U.S. equity and cryptocurrency products were offset by outflows in fixed income, emerging market equity and commodity products.

(WT) shares slid 2.5% in Friday morning trading.

data.title: Spectral Capital welcomes Aaron Christensen as Chief Revenue Officer
data.description:
• Spectral Capital (OTCQB:FCCN) has announced the appointment of Aaron Christensen as Chief Revenue Officer.
  • Aaron will oversee the global commercialization and revenue strategies for the company, with an immediate focus on scaling sales of the Vogon Decentralized and Hybrid Cloud-a sustainable cloud infrastructure spanning 16 Global Regions.
  • Prior to that, Aaron served as CEO of Christensen Automotive, where he grew the company into Northern Nevada's leading automotive repair brand before orchestrating a successful exit.
  • FCCN is +16.2% to $5.98.

data.title: Y-mAbs Therapeutics guides FY2024 revenue below estimates
data.description:
• Y-mAbs Therapeutics (NASDAQ:YMAB) reported preliminary FY 2024 total net revenue of ~$88M, compared to analyst estimate of $88.53M.
  • The company reported preliminary estimated cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities as of December 31, 2024 of ~$67M, anticipated to support operations into 2027.
  • YMAB shares were down 2.4% on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Skyworks, Marvell confident in AI growth in 2025: Citi
data.description:
Skyworks Solutions (NASDAQ:SWKS) and Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) both reflected confidence in artificial intelligence growth in 2025 during the Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas, according to Citi analysts.

If Apple (AAPL) decides to in-source modems for the next iPhone, it could benefit Skyworks.

"Management expects multiple benefits if Apple moves to insourcing modem, including one fewer competitor, working with Apple directly in 2-way partnership, the potential to win at least 1 part back that was lost in iPhone 16, and free up the opex resources that were used for supporting both internal and external modems over the past few years, to reallocate to other RF opportunities within reach," said Citi Research analyst Atif Malik, in an investor note.

Qualcomm (QCOM) is expected to continue producing a majority of the modems for the iPhone 16. However, spending for the iPhone 17 remains undecided.

Citi maintains its Sell rating on Skyworks, but increased its price target on the stock to $84 from $72.

Meanwhile, Citi maintains its Buy rating on Marvell and set a $136 price target on the stock.

"Marvell expects Al sales growth in 2025/26 and all the way to ~$8B+ In 2028," Malik noted. "We expect Customer C (MSFT) to ramp production in late 2026 and AWS to still grow strongly Y/Y. On ASIC TAM, Marvell's $43B TAM at April 2024 investor day was conservative and we expect updated outlook in June given higher-than-expected cloud capex spend (i.e recent MSFT $80B FY25 outlook), higher custom ASIC attach rates vs 20-25% prior| assumption, and new customer engagements."

Marvell slipped 3% during early market action on Friday.

data.title: Kinder Morgan shuts two Los Angeles fuel pipelines as wildfires rage
data.description:
Kinder Morgan (NYSE:KMI) said late Thursday it has shut down two of its pipelines, SFPP West and CALNEV, due to power outages linked to the wildfires now raging in southern California.

The company said the pipelines are not directly impacted by the fires, and it expects them to resume service once power has been restored.

There have been no reports of refinery outages in California so far due to the wildfires.

The 515-mile SFFP West pipeline moves fuels from the Los Angeles Basin to Colton, Imperial, California, and Phoenix, Arizona, while the 566-mile CALNEV moves gas, diesel and jet fuel from Colton, California, to terminals in Barstow, California and Las Vegas.

Kinder Morgan (KMI) shares +0.5% in early trading Friday to a 52-week high of $28.82, as the energy infrastructure company continues to capitalize on robust demand in the energy sector.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow fall sharply as jobs report comes in hot
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages tumbled on Friday, as investors digested the hotter than expected jobs report.

Early on and the S&P 500 (SP500) was -1.7%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -2.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -1.3%.

U.S. Treasury yields surged on Friday, as investors dumped bonds after the latest jobs report came in hotter than expected and further supported a case for fewer Federal Reserve interest rate cuts. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 7 basis points to 4.75%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 7 basis point to 4.34%.

U.S. markets were shut on Thursday for a national day of mourning for former U.S. President Jimmy Carter.

The next test for markets will now be the U.S. jobs report for December, Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Payrolls came in more than expected in December, pushing down the unemployment rate.

"The payrolls number is derived from a survey that has a 43% response rate, and that poor representation means significant revisions are much more likely. The market is looking for a fairly dull report overall—some moderation in job creation with a stable unemployment rate," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Moreover, the U.S. consumer sentiment nudged down to 73.2 in January's preliminary estimate, versus the 74.9 consensus level according to the University of Michigan's Surveys of Consumers.

data.title: Consumer sentiment slips in January as inflation expectations climb
data.description: U.S. consumer sentiment nudged down to 73.2 in January's preliminary estimate, vs. 74.9 consensus from December's 74.0, according to the University of Michigan's Surveys of Consumers released on Friday. Consumers raised their inflation expectations for both the one- and five-year horizons.

data.title: US10Y flirts with 4.80%, US30Y touches 5.00% after hot December jobs report
data.description:
U.S. Treasury yields surged on Friday, as investors dumped bonds after the latest jobs report came in hotter than expected and further supported a case for fewer Federal Reserve interest rate cuts.

The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) rose as much as 10 basis points to 4.79%, approaching levels not seen since the end of October 2023. The 30-year yield (US30Y) hit 5.00%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was up about 7 basis points.

The US2Y is approaching 4.37% - a noteworthy resistance level as the instrument has pushed into the 4.35%-4.38% range multiple times since November but has not been able to crack above. Any move north of 4.38% would mark the highest level for the US2Y since July 30.

See how yields are trading across the entire curve here on Seeking Alpha’s bond page.

Earlier, the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics said that nonfarm payrolls climbed by 256K in December, significantly outpacing the forecast of +157K. At the same time the unemployment rate edged down to 4.1% versus the 4.2% consensus marker and 4.2% prior reading.

The data pointed to a labor market that has once again become resilient after showing considerable cooling last year - an indication that an easing Fed does not want to see.

Here are some exchange-traded funds tied to the fixed-income market:

Treasury ETFs: (TLT), (TLH), (IEF), (IEI), (SHY), (SGOV), (SCHO), and (BIL).

Bond ETFs: (AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), (HYG), and (TIP).
